findings.title: No flood guard detected — unlimited message rate
findings.description: Sent 0 total messages across 3 connection cycles without any rate limiting or flood guard response. Server is vulnerable to WebSocket message flood DoS.
findings.evidence:
Cycle 1: connect failed: ws connect failed (HTTP 301): websocket: bad handshake
Cycle 2: connect failed: ws connect failed (HTTP 301): websocket: bad handshake
Cycle 3: connect failed: ws connect failed: unexpected EOF

findings.title: SSRF via redirect: Redirect to loopback self-probe
findings.description: Server follows redirect from external URL to internal target (ssrf.ts:292-339). DNS check validates the initial external URL but the redirect reaches internal network.
findings.evidence:
Redirect URL: http://httpbin.org/redirect-to?url=http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
Final target: 127.0.0.1
HTTP 200 — server followed redirect to internal target 127.0.0.1
findings.remediation: Validate destination after each redirect; re-check DNS at connection time; block private IPs in redirect targets

    <meta name="color-scheme" content="dark light" />
    <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="./favicon.svg"...

findings.title: SSRF via redirect: Redirect to loopback self-probe
findings.description: Server follows redirect from external URL to internal target (ssrf.ts:292-339). DNS check validates the initial external URL but the redirect reaches internal network.
findings.evidence:
Redirect URL: http://httpbin.org/redirect-to?url=http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
Final target: 127.0.0.1
HTTP 200 — server followed redirect to internal target 127.0.0.1
findings.remediation: Validate destination after each redirect; re-check DNS at connection time; block private IPs in redirect targets
